behaviors.description: Products marked as out-of-stock are excluded from the recommendation results regardless of their relevance score.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is filter-to-ranker handoff (availability filter must dominate relevance score), which is beyond a single function. Rejected Unit because the behavior explicitly depends on interaction between stock filtering and ranked output. Rejected System/Agentic/Workflow because behavior text only states local filtering/ranking logic, with no required real OS/network/subprocess or model-quality dependence.
behaviors.test_description: Wire catalog filter + ranking modules with an in-process catalog fixture containing high-score out-of-stock items; assert none appear in final ranked page.

behaviors.description: The user preference filter correctly excludes product categories the user has explicitly blocked in their settings.
behaviors.justification: Bug appears when user-settings module and catalog filtering module interact. Rejected Unit because blocked-category state must be handed off across modules. Rejected System/Agentic/Workflow since no real infra or AI decision quality is required by the behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Use real preference-store adapter (local/in-memory DB) plus filter pipeline; set blocked categories and verify excluded products never enter recommendation candidates.

behaviors.description: The recommendation algorithm ranks products that match the user's recent browsing patterns higher than products with no browsing signal.
behaviors.justification: This is ranking-quality behavior of the recommendation model/algorithm output itself, not just call plumbing. A stubbed scorer could always satisfy expected ordering and hide real model/scoring failures. Rejected Workflow because it is a single ranking decision, not multiple agentic decisions in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Run the real recommender scoring path on controlled user-history fixtures; compare ranked positions of browsing-matched vs no-signal products across repeated trials and assert matched items are consistently prioritized.

behaviors.description: Pagination returns the correct page of results, with stable ordering — requesting page 2 twice returns identical results if the underlying data hasn't changed.
behaviors.justification: Requires sorter + paginator + state snapshot interaction; real failure is module handoff (stable order before slicing). Rejected Unit because single-function pagination tests can miss instability introduced upstream. Rejected System/Agentic/Workflow because behavior text is deterministic local data handling with no real infra or model-quality dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Generate a fixed recommendation list through pipeline, request page 2 twice with unchanged fixtures, assert identical item IDs/order and correct offset/limit boundaries.

behaviors.description: The recommendation algorithm surfaces at least one product from a category the user hasn't browsed before (serendipity requirement), avoiding a filter bubble.
behaviors.justification: Serendipity is output-quality behavior of recommender decisions; deterministic mocks can trivially inject one unseen category and mask real model/exploration failures. Rejected Workflow because this is one recommendation decision, not a multi-step agent pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Exercise real recommendation generation on users with narrow history and broad catalog; assert top-N contains at least one item from an unseen category under production scoring/exploration settings.

behaviors.description: A new user with no purchase or browsing history receives recommendations based on overall popularity rather than an empty result set.
behaviors.justification: Failure is at cold-start fallback orchestration: empty-history detection + popularity retriever + ranking pipeline. Rejected Unit because it depends on cross-module fallback behavior. Rejected Agentic because behavior text specifies deterministic fallback source ('overall popularity') rather than model-quality judgment.
behaviors.test_description: Run end-to-end in-process recommendation flow for a user with no history; assert non-empty results and that items come from configured popularity source.

behaviors.description: Click tracking records the correct product ID, position in the list, and timestamp for each recommendation the user interacts with.
behaviors.justification: Real failure is event payload handoff from recommendation response context to tracking persistence. Rejected Unit because isolated serializer tests miss incorrect position/context wiring. Rejected System because local DB/event sink can reproduce the boundary; no required OS/network behavior in text.
behaviors.test_description: Serve recommendations, simulate click on a specific rank, persist via real tracking module to local store, and assert recorded product_id, rank position, and event timestamp fields.

behaviors.description: The response time for generating recommendations stays under 200ms for a catalog of 100,000 products.
behaviors.justification: This is a performance SLO tied to real runtime behavior (process, memory, CPU, storage access patterns). In-process mocks hide latency and contention. Rejected Integration because mocked/local simplified infra would not expose true timing failures.
behaviors.test_description: Run the recommendation service in a production-like process configuration against a 100k-product dataset; measure p95 end-to-end generation latency and assert <200ms.

behaviors.description: When a recommended product is purchased by the user, the recommendation algorithm deprioritizes it in subsequent requests.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is state propagation: purchase event ingestion to next recommendation ranking. Rejected Unit because bug commonly appears in cross-module state handoff. Rejected Workflow because only one model/ranking step is asserted after one state update, not multiple agentic stages.
behaviors.test_description: Generate recommendations, mark one recommended item as purchased via purchase pipeline, request recommendations again, and assert that item’s rank drops (or is excluded) in subsequent output.

behaviors.description: The recommendation results are deterministic for the same user state — requesting recommendations twice with no intervening user activity returns the same ranked list.
behaviors.justification: Determinism failure appears when the full ranking pipeline (feature fetch, tie-break/sort, pagination seed usage) is wired together. Rejected Unit because single-function determinism can pass while pipeline introduces nondeterministic ordering. Rejected Workflow because this is repeated single-step generation, not multi-step agentic chaining.
behaviors.test_description: Freeze user/profile/catalog state, call recommendation endpoint twice in-process with identical inputs and no writes between calls, assert exact ranked-list equality.
